figures.caption: TABLE III. Calculated orbital-dependent quasiparticle weights Z, effective electron masses m∗ and fitting parameter α for dhcp and fcc phases of Am, Cm, Bk and Cf at T = 290 K.
TABLE IV. Calculated kinetic energy (Ekin), potential energy (Epot), spin susceptibility (χS0) and charge susceptibility (χD) for dhcp and fcc phases of Am, Cm, Bk and Cf at T = 290 K.

FIG. 6. (Color online) (a)–(d) Valence state histograms of dhcp phase for Am, Cm, Bk and Cf at T = 290 K by the DFT + DMFT method. The atomic eigenstates are denoted by using good quantum numbers N (total occupancy), J (total angular momentum) and γ (the rest of the good quantum numbers), i.e., |ψΓ⟩ = |N, J, γ⟩. The distributions of atomic eigenstate probabilities with respect to different N are displayed in the legends.

FIG. 7. (Color online) (a)–(d) Valence state histograms of fcc phase for Am, Cm, Bk and Cf at T = 290 K by the DFT + DMFT method. The atomic eigenstates are denoted by using good quantum numbers N (total occupancy), J (total angular momentum) and γ (the rest of the good quantum numbers), i.e., |ψΓ⟩ = |N, J, γ⟩. The distributions of atomic eigenstate probabilities with respect to different N are displayed in the legends.

FIG. 8. (Color online) Imaginary parts of the Matsubara self-energy functions of Am, Cm, Bk and Cf at T = 290 K in the low-frequency regime by DFT + DMFT calculations. (a) For the 5 f j = 5/2 states of dhcp phase. (b) For the 5 f j = 7/2 states of dhcp phase. (c) For the 5 f j = 5/2 states of fcc phase. (d) For the 5 f j = 7/2 states of fcc phase.

TABLE II. The weights for 5 f electronic configurations w(5 f n), 5 f orbital occupancy (n5 f , n5/2 and n7/2), total angular momentum ⟨J⟩, valence state fluctuation strength V and x-ray absorption branching ratio B for fcc phase for Am, Cm, Bk and Cf at T = 290 K.
